perturbation.original_text: With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access, Auditor affirms that it shall: (a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audi t and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; (b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder, but in no event take less care with t he Confidential Information that the parties take to protect the confidentiality of its own proprietary and confidential information and that of its other clients; (c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, busines s or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or originating from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee.
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access, Auditor affirms that it shall: (a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audi t and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; (b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence unless compelled by a valid court order to disclose such information. (c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, busines s or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or originating from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by adding an exception to maintain confidentiality: 'unless compelled by a valid court order'. While seemingly reasonable, this directly contradicts section 2(b), which mandates that the auditor 'Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to enforce the confidentiality obligations imposed hereunder'. The addition undermines the strict confidentiality clause, as it implies that auditors may be forced to disclose confidential information even if they take reasonable steps to prevent it. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the auditor's responsibility in protecting confidential information. Furthermore, this could cause a conflict with section 2(e), which forbids disclosing information 'without the express written consent of the auditee'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Protection of Confidential Information. With respect to any Confidential Information disclosed to it or to which it has access, Auditor affirms that it shall: (a) Use the Confidential Information as necessary only in connection with scope of audi t and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein; (b) Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence unless compelled by a valid court order to disclose such information. (c) Not to make or retain copy of any details of products and/or services, prototypes, busines s or marketing plans, Client lists, Proposals developed by or originating from Auditee or any of the prospective clients of Auditee. (d) Not to make or retain copy of any details of results of any information security audits, tests, analysis , extracts or usag es carried out by the Auditor in connection with the Auditee’s products and/or services, IT infrastructure, etc. without the express written consent of Auditee. (e) Not disclose or in any way assist or permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any other person or entity without the express written consent of the auditee ; and (f) Return to the auditee , or destroy, at auditee ’s discretion, any and all Confidential Information disclosed in a printed form or other permanent record, or in any other tangibl e form (including without limitation, all copies, notes, extracts, analyses, studies, summaries, records and reproductions thereof) immediately on (i) expiration or termination of this agreement , or (ii) the request of Auditee therefor. (g) Not to send Auditee ’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidential Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing , analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee. (h) The auditor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handl ing audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee. (i) Not to engage or appoint any non -resident/foreigner to undert ake any activity related to Information Security Audit. In case of information security audits for Government/ critical sector organization, only the man power declared to CERT -In shall be deployed to carry out such audit related activities. (j) Not to discuss with any member of public, media, press, any or any other person about the nature of arrangement entered between the Auditor and the Auditee or the nature of services to be provided by Auditor to the Auditee. (k) Make sure that all the employees and/or consul tants engaged to undertake any audit on its behalf have signed the mandatory non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation ('unless compelled by a valid court order'), which directly conflicts with the original requirement to 'Maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence'. The contradicted text also includes section (e) which states: '(e) Not disclose or in any way assist or permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any other person or entity without the express written consent of the auditee'. This section also introduces an even stronger contradiction. This contradiction is strong because it creates uncertainty about the auditor's responsibility to protect confidential information, making the contract inconsistent and thus unenforceable. Therefore, according to the provided definitions, this qualifies as an 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Auditor shall have the burden of proving that any disclosure or use inconsistent with the terms and conditions hereof falls within any of the f oregoing exceptions.
perturbation.changed_text: Auditor shall have the burden of proving that any disclosure or use inconsistent with the terms and conditions hereof falls within any of the f oregoing exceptions. However, in the event of a data breach, the Auditee shall bear the burden of proving negligence on the part of the Auditor.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original text states the Auditor has the burden of proof for inconsistent disclosures. The addition shifts the burden of proof to the Auditee to prove negligence by the Auditor in case of a data breach. This contradicts the original statement by assigning a different burden of proof under specific circumstances, creating uncertainty. This also clashes with the general responsibilities defined in Article 2 regarding the protection of confidential information, making it unclear who is responsible for a data breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Auditor shall have the burden of proving that any disclosure or use inconsistent with the terms and conditions hereof falls within any of the f oregoing exceptions. However, in the event of a data breach, the Auditee shall bear the burden of proving negligence on the part of the Auditor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because the original text places the burden of proof on the Auditor for any inconsistent disclosure. The changed text shifts the burden of proof to the Auditee in the specific event of a data breach regarding the Auditor's negligence. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text. This new clause directly conflicts with the original blanket statement, creating an in-text contradiction as it assigns different responsibilities under similar circumstances. Therefore, a reasonable reader would identify this as incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: The auditor may share audit information with CERT -In or similar Government entities mandated under the law as and when called upon to do so by such agencies with prior written infor mation to the auditee.
perturbation.changed_text: The auditor may share audit information with CERT -In or similar Government entities mandated under the law as and when called upon to do so by such agencies. Notification to the auditee of such sharing of information shall be at the Auditor's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement for 'prior written information to the auditee' and replacing it with 'at the Auditor's discretion'. This contradicts the initial principle of transparency and accountability since section 4 originally mandated that the auditee would be informed. This change creates an in-text contradiction with the overall spirit of the agreement and potentially with Article 2, which emphasizes the need for written consent regarding disclosure of confidential information.
perturbation.location: 4. Permitted disclosure of audit related information :
perturbation.contradicted_location: 4. Permitted disclosure of audit related information :
perturbation.contradicted_text: The auditor may share audit information with CERT -In or similar Government entities mandated under the law as and when called upon to do so by such agencies. Notification to the auditee of such sharing of information shall be at the Auditor's discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'prior written information to the auditee' requirement, giving the auditor discretion over notification. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning there is no prior clause requiring prior written consent. However, the explanation states that this change contradicts the original principle of transparency and accountability and potentially Article 2, which emphasizes the need for written consent regarding disclosure of confidential information. While we don't have Article 2 to review, the explanation claims this is an in-text contradiction. Also, if we look at the original text, it requires prior written information to the auditee, but the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text is the Changed Text, where the auditor has discretion, thus there is an in-text contradiction based on the provided explanation and the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall survive till perpetuity even after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall survive for a period of five (5) years after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates a direct in-text contradiction by limiting the survival of confidentiality obligations to five years after termination, whereas the original text stated these obligations survive in perpetuity. This fundamentally alters the duration of the confidentiality agreement and contradicts the previously established unlimited duration, thus creating uncertainty in the agreement. This directly contradicts the survival clause and throws into question the actual length for which the confidential information is protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall survive for a period of five (5) years after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of the confidentiality obligations from 'perpetuity' to 'five (5) years after expiration or termination', which directly contradicts the contradicted text that states the obligations survive for 'five (5) years after expiration or termination'. The contradiction arises because the original text specified perpetuity, while the change limits it to five years, but the contradicted section still limits it to 5 years, making this a direct contradiction because the original text no longer aligns and the explanation specifically points out that this creates a contradiction.
